Exclusive to:

WX-78's Chassis Body

Circuits are WX-78-exclusive items craftable in the Survivor Items Filter in Don't Starve Together. All the circuits are unlocked by scanning certain mobs with Bio Scanalyzer. They can be categorized into three types: Alpha (red), Beta (blue) and Gamma (yellow). Each circuit corresponds for which bar it belongs to, based on what shapes are on top of the head, the color slab and pins.

When a Circuit is applied to the bar, it will attempt to take up the lowest unoccupied pins available. Removing a Circuit via Circuit Extractor will remove the highest Circuit and lose 25% durability.

When hovering over the charge meter, it will show which circuits are currently plugged in. The charge meter affects all three bars; when charge is gained or lost then this will activate or deactivate all three circuits in line with that charge.

With "Right To Modify" skill, WX-78 can remove any circuits in the bar stack. If two of the same circuits are next to each other, only the top one is able to be extracted. When extracted, circuits lose 12.5% instead of 25%.

With "Watts Up" skill, Circuits with 2 or more notches will take one less charge when extracted. Also, charge regenerate 3 times faster, taking thirty seconds per charge.

With "Off By One" skill, the Alpha, Beta and Gamma bars get an additional +1 slot each.

Hardy Circuit

“Plugs into WX78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Spider.

Requires 1 socket and raises Maximum Health +40.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to provide 2.5% armor.”

-

The Hardy Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Spider. Plugging in a Hardy Circuit uses a single slot and increases maximum health by 40.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill, each Hardy Circuit grants 2.5% protection. Note that this only reduces physical damage taken.

Usage

×2

×4

Super-Hardy Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Nurse Spider.

Requires 2 sockets and increases Maximum Health by 100 points.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to provide 5% armor.”

-

The Super-Hardy Circuit is an upgraded version of the Hardy Circuit. The Super-Hardy Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Nurse Spider. Plugging in a Super-Hardy Circuit uses two slots and increases maximum health by 100.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill, each Super-Hardy Circuit grants 5% protection. Note that this only reduces physical damage taken.

Gastrogain Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a regular Hound.

Requires 1 socket and increases Maximum Hunger by 40 points.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to reduce Hunger drain by 5%.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts the Hunger drain reduction to 10%.”

-

The Gastrogain Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Hound. Plugging in a Gastrogain Circuit uses one slot and increases maximum hunger by 40.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering I" skill, each Gastrogain Circuit reduces hunger drain by 5%. Multiple circuits stack multiplicatively.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill, each Gastrogain Circuit reduces hunger drain by 10%. Multiple circuits stack multiplicatively.

Usage

×3

Super-Gastrogain Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Slurper, or Bearger.

Requires 2 sockets, increases Maximum Hunger by 100 points and reduces Hunger drain by 20%.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I boosts the Hunger drain reduction to 25%.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts the Hunger drain reduction to 30%.”

-

The Super-Gastrogain Circuit is an upgraded version of the Gastrogain Circuit, and can be crafted after scanning a Slurper or Bearger. Plugging in a Super-Gastrogain Circuit uses two slots and increases maximum hunger by 100, also slowing hunger drain by 20%

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering I" skill, each Super-Gastrogain Circuit reduces hunger drain by 25%. Multiple circuits stack multiplicatively.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill, each Super-Gastrogain Circuit reduces hunger drain by 30%. Multiple circuits stack multiplicatively.

Processing Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Butterfly, or Moon Moth.

Requires 1 socket and increases Maximum Sanity +40.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to have a 80% modifier to negative sanity auras.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts the circuit to increase the sanity gain of clothing items by 10%.”

-

The Processing Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Butterfly or a Moon Moth. Plugging in a Processing Circuit uses a single slot and increases maximum sanity by 40.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering I" skill each Processing Circuit have a 20% resistance to negative sanity aura.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill each Processing Circuit boosts sanity gain from clothing items by 10%.

Usage

×3

Super-Processing Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from Shadow Creatures.

Requires 2 sockets and increases Maximum Sanity +100.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to have a 50% modifier to negative sanity auras.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts the circuit to increase the sanity gain of clothing items by 30%.”

-

The Super-Processing Circuit is an upgraded version of the Processing Circuit, and can be crafted after scanning a Shadow Creature. Plugging in a Super-Processing circuit uses two slots and increases maximum sanity by 100.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering I" skill each Super-Processing Circuit have a 50% resistance to negative sanity auras.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill each Super-Processing Circuit boosts sanity gain from clothing items by 30%.

Usage

×8

Beanbooster Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Alpha circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from Bee Queen.

Requires 3 sockets, heals 5 Health every 30 seconds, gains 2 Sanity every minute and increases Maximum Sanity by 100 points.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to have a 50% modifier to negative sanity auras.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II boosts the circuit to increase the sanity gain of clothing items by 30%, and provides a regenerating shield when WX-78 is at full health. The shield protects an attack from damaging its health if WX-78 has over 15 shield.”

-

The Beanbooster Circuit is an upgrade Circuit that can only be crafted by WX-78. It requires 8 Bio Data, 1 Royal Jelly and 1 Super-Processing Circuit to craft. The Bee Queen must be scanned by a Bio Scanalyzer before WX-78 can craft it. Plugging in a Beanbooster Circuit uses three slots.

While it is powered, 5 are regenerated every 30 seconds, 2 are regenerated every minute and WX-78's maximum sanity is increased by 100.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering I" skill having at least one Beanbooster Circuit gives a 50% resistance to negative sanity aura.

Alpha Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Alpha Circuits Tinkering II" skill having at least one Beanbooster Circuit provides a sanity gain from clothing by 30%, and gives a shield when WX-78 is at full health. When the shield is over 15 points, WX-78 gets a one shot protection from incoming damage. The shield regenerates for 0.25 points/second (or 15 points/min). It doesn't regenerate during combat.

Rangebooster Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Crow, Redbird, Snowbird, Puffin or Canary.

Requires 1 socket and increases WX-78's Vision Range.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to increase the range of the Roto-Mapper, Zaptrocuter, Bio Scanalyzer scan area, and W.A.R.B.I.S target locking.”

-

The Rangebooster Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Redbird, a Snowbird, a Crow, a Puffin, or a Canary. The Rangebooster Circuit uses one slot and plugs into the Beta circuit board.

The Rangebooster Circuit can expand range of vision.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill, the Rangebooster Circuits increases the radius of their drones (Bio Scanalyzer, Roto-Mapper, Zaptrocuter, Auto-Grabber, Exploiterator), and increases the range of W.A.R.B.I.S. Head Gear and W.A.R.B.I.S. Armor target locking.

Chorusbox Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from Crabby Hermit, or the Crab King.

Requires 3 sockets, raises Sanity of nearby survivors 5 points a minute and raises garden plant happiness from the music.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to recruit Pig Men and Bunny Men who hear the song.”

-

The Chorusbox Circuit can be crafted after scanning Crab King or the Crabby Hermit. Plugging in a Chorusbox Circuit uses three slots and plays a faint melody, giving a sanity aura of +4.44 per minute and tending Farm Plants within 3 tiles.

Plugging in one Chorusbox Circuit will play the main music box part of the Ragtime song below, with an additional Chorusbox Circuit adding the backing drums.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" having at least one Chorusbox Circuit recruits nearby mobs such as Pig or Bunnyman to be followers. A new chorus layer will also be played along with normal Chorusbox Melody.

Sounds

Chorusbox Melody (1 Chorusbox Circuit)
Chorusbox Melody (2 Chorusbox Circuit)
Chorusbox Melody (with second Chorusbox Circuit)
Bunnymen (with Beta Circuits Tinkering I skill)

Acceleration Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Rabbit.

Requires 6 socket and raises movement speed by 25%.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to reduce slowness effects by 25%.”

-

The Acceleration Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Rabbit. Plugging in an Acceleration Circuit uses all six slots and increases movement speed by 25%.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering II" each Acceleration Circuit reduces the speed penalty caused by carrying heavy objects, Sticky Webbing and Bee Queen's sticky trail by 25%.

Usage

×6

Super-Acceleration Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Rook, or the Ancient Guardian.

Requires 2 sockets. The first raises movement speed by 25%. The second raises movement speed by 15%. The third raises movement speed by 10%. The maximum speed boost can be 50%.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to reduce slowness effects by 25%.”

-

The Super-Acceleration Circuit is an upgraded version of the Acceleration Circuit, and can be crafted after scanning a Clockwork Rook, Damaged Rook, or the Ancient Guardian. Plugging in a Super-Acceleration Circuit uses two slots and increases movement speed by 25%. Plugging in a second Super-Acceleration Circuit will provide an additional 15% movement speed while a third will provide an additional 10% movement speed, totaling for a possible increase in 50% movement speed.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering II" each Acceleration Circuit reduces the speed penalty caused by carrying heavy objects, Sticky Webbing and Bee Queen's sticky trail by 25% (50% with two, 75% with three).

Thermal Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Fire Hound, Geothermite, or Dragonfly.

Requires 3 sockets and raises body temperature 20 degrees, increases spoil rate of inventory by 25% and increases drying rate by 10%. Will also warm nearby survivors.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to provide freezing resistance, and grants full immunity to freezing when two are plugged.”

-

The Thermal Circuit can be crafted after scanning Dragonfly, Red Hound or a Geothermite. Plugging in a Thermal Circuit uses three slots.

The effects of the Thermal Circuit include: increasing the player's minimum body temperature by 20 degrees, providing a heating effect to players around them by 25 degrees, increasing inventory spoil rate by 25%, and increasing the rate drying off wetness by 10%.

The default minimum temperature is -20, meaning just one Thermal Circuit prevents WX-78 from going below 0 degrees, making them safe from ambient freezing.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill one Thermal Circuit reduces the damage of Freezing by 50%, up to 100% with two circuits.

Refrigerant Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from an Ice Hound, or Deerclops.

Requires 3 sockets and lowers body temperature 20 degrees and decreases spoil rate of inventory by 25%. Will also cool nearby survivors.

If WX-78's wetness reaches 95%, it will drop to 0% and 2 Ice will be spawned.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to provide 50% fire damage resistance.”

-

The Refrigerant Circuit can be crafted after scanning Deerclops or a Blue Hound. Plugging in a Refrigerant Circuit uses three slots.

The effects of the Refrigerant Circuit include reducing the player's maximum body temperature by 20 degrees, providing a cool down effect to players around them by 25 degrees and reducing inventory spoil rate by 25%.

Upon reaching 95 Wetness the moisture solidifies resulting in 2 Ice spawning and resetting wetness back to 0. This can improve WX-78's odds of survival after drowning in the ocean.

The default maximum temperature is 90, meaning just one Refrigerant Circuit prevents WX-78 from going above 70 degrees, making them safe from ambient overheating.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill one Refrigerant Circuit reduces the damage of Overheating or Burning damage by 50%, up to 100% with two circuits.

Electrification Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Volt Goat.

Requires 2 sockets and deals 20 electrical damage to WX-78's attackers.

This module also makes WX-78 immune to electrical shock.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to allow WX-78 to get overloaded when being hit, culminating in a 50 electrical damage ASoE blast when hit enough times.”

-

The Electrification Circuit is an item that can only be crafted by WX-78, exclusive to Don't Starve Together. It can be crafted after scanning a Volt Goat. Plugging in an Electrification Circuit uses two slots.

With an Electrification Circuit equipped, WX-78 will do damage to an attacker when they are hit, subject to a cooldown of 0.3 seconds. The damage has a base value of 20 (40 with two, 60 with three), but is electric, dealing more damage if the target does not have immunity to electricity (1.5×, or 30/60/90 damage, to dry mobs; 2.5×, or 50/100/150 damage, to wet mobs). This counterattack will also charge an attacking Volt Goat.

Additionally, WX-78 gains immunity to electricity with this circuit equipped.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering II" having at least one Electrification Circuit will burst out a base 50 AoE Electric Damage (100 with two, 150 with three) when WX-78 gets hit 10 times. *Damage varied on difference circumstances.

Illumination Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from Fireflies.

Requires 3 sockets and creates light.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to provide a directional cone of light facing WX-78's direction.”

-

The Illumination Circuit can be crafted after scanning Fireflies. It uses three slot and plugs into the Beta circuit board.

The Illumination Circuit provides a small light radius around WX-78 while equipped.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering II" skill, having at least one Illumination Circuit gives WX-78 a flash light in the direction they face. Plugging in more Illumination Circuit and Super-Illumination Circuit increases the radius of the light and its cone.

Super-Illumination Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Depth Worm, Bulbous Lightbug, or Skittersquid.

Requires 1 socket and creates light.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II boosts this circuit to provide a directional cone of light facing WX-78's direction.”

-

The Super-Illumination Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Depths Worm, a Bulbous Lightbug, or a Skittersquid. It uses one slot and plugs into the Beta circuit board.

The Super-Illumination Circuit provides a small light radius around WX-78 while equipped.

Beta Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering II" skill, having at least one Super-Illumination gives WX-78 a flash light in the direction they face. Plugging in more Illumination Circuit or Super-Illumination Circuit increases the radius of the light and its cone.

Optoelectronic Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Moleworm.

Requires 4 sockets and lets the user see in the dark. Turns on at night and when entering the Caves.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I boosts this circuit to not have mole vision any longer.”

-

The Optoelectronic Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Moleworm. Plugging in an Optoelectronic Circuit uses four slots and lets the user see in the dark. It automatically activates at night and when entering a cave.

Beta Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Beta Circuits Tinkering I" skill one Optoelectronic Circuit removes the Moggles filter.

Spatializer Circuit

HELDNOPOWERGENERIC

“BOUNDLESS SPACE”

–WX-78

“ACCESS DENIED. INSUFFICIENT POWER”

–WX-78

“RECOVERY MODE”

–WX-78

“Plugs into WX-78's Beta circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from Krampus.

Requires 1 socket and places a infinite stack-size 1 slot container in WX-78's right-most inventory slot.”

-

The Spatializer Circuit can be crafted after scanning Krampus. The Spatializer Circuit uses one slot and plugs into the Beta circuit board.

When plugged in and powered, the Spatializer Circuit replaces the right-most inventory slot with a Expansive Storage Unit, which is an infinite stack-size one slot container.

If WX-78 loses charge of the Spatializer Circuit, the Expansive Storage Unit will close until powered again.

Redigestion Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Gamma circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Catcoon.

Requires 2 sockets and allows WX-78 to process spoiled foods, such as Rot, to bake Nutribricks.

Gamma Circuit Tinkering I allows WX-78 to also process nearly spoiled foods to bake Nutribricks.”

-

The Redigestion Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Catcoon. The Redigestion Circuit uses two slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board. With a single Circuit plugged in, when WX-78 consumes 5 Rot, Rotten Egg, Spoiled Fish or Spoiled Fish Morsel, they bake a Nutribrick. The cost can be reduced by 1 for each additional circuit plugged, down to 3 items to produce a Nutribrick with 3 Redigestion Circuits.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering I", Redigestion Circuit can process food when it's Spoiled (red value), and contribute to baking the Nutribrick. Additionally, when spoiled, foods no longer give negative stats when processed.

Chessmaster Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Gamma circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Knight.

Requires 1 socket and allows WX-78 to have 1 additional Clockwork follower. Interacting with Clockworks no longer requires them to be stunned.

Gamma Circuit Tinkering I allows WX-78 to approach Clockworks without drawing aggro.”

-

The Chessmaster Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Clockwork Knight, Damaged Knight, or Gilded Knight. The Chessmaster Circuit uses a single slot and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.

Without Chessmaster Circuit, WX-78 can recruit 9 total clockworks, and max cap per type is 3.

Each Chessmaster Circuit increases +1 total clockworks.

Every 3 Chessmaster Circuits increases +1 max cap per type.

  • 1-3 circuits => max cap per type is 4.
  • 4-6 circuits => max cap per type is 5.
  • 7 circuits => max cap per type is 6.

With 7 circuits, WX-78 can recruit a total of 9+7=16 clockworks, but no more than 6 of one type.  E.g.

  • 6 bishops, 6 rooks, 4 knights
  • Or 6 bishops, 5 rooks, 5 knights

Extra clockworks can be recruited, but they will stay in place and wait. Once WX-78 has less than cap amount of clockworks, they will wake up and follow WX-78.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering I" skill, plugging in a Chessmaster Circuit causes all Clockwork enemies to no longer attack unless attacked first.

Sonic-Invoker Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Gamma circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Naked Mole Bat.

Requires 3 sockets and allows WX-78 to perform a sonic attack, inflicting panic on nearby creatures, and going off cooldown for 20 seconds.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering I allows WX-78 to hold the sonic attack.”

-

The Sonic-Invoker Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Naked Mole Bat. The Sonic-Invoker Circuit uses three slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.

When plugged in and powered, WX-78 can right-click to initiate sonic waves for a short time. It will make mobs within 3 Tiles to panic for 8 seconds, but has no effect to boss. This skill has 20 seconds cooldown.

Plugging in the second Sonic-Invoker Circuit will expand the distance to 6 Tiles.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering I

For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering I", sonic waves can be played indefinitely until the player moves and cancels it.

Blocking Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Gamma circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Rock Lobster, Slurtle, or Snurtle.

Requires 4 sockets and allows WX-78 to perform a block, providing 80% armor, going off cooldown for 20 seconds.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering II provides WX-78 with knockback immunity in the block.”

-

The Blocking Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Slurtle, a Snurtle, or a Rock Lobster. The Blocking Circuit uses four slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.

When plugged in and powered, player can right-click to block. This will provide 80% defense and protect them against thief sources such as Frogs, Bearger, Powder Monkey, Otter, etc.

Blocking can be ended when WX-78 moves or it forcibly ends once more than 100 total damage has been taken (i.e. WX-78 loses more than 100 health). Additionally, taking damage or blocking for more than 5 seconds causes the ability to enter a 20-second cooldown.

This 80% protection only counts for Physical Damage. When paired with other armor, the armor takes damage first, and the rest of damage is further reduced by the Blocking Circuit. For example, an spider deals 20 damage. When WX-78 is blocking with Blocking Circuit and a Thulecite Suit:

  • 20 * 0.9 = 18: Thulecite Suit reduces 18 durability.
  • (20 - 18) * (1 - 0.8) = 0.4: WX-78 loses 0.4 health.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering II", Blocking Circuit gives immunity to Knockback.

Spin-Cycle Circuit

“Plugs into WX-78's Gamma circuit board. Crafting recipe is scanned from a Mosling.

Requires 3 sockets and allows WX-78 to perform a spin when using any Axe or Pickaxe when working or attacking.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering II makes WX-78 spin faster.”

-

The Spin-Cycle Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Mosling. The Spin-Cycle Circuit uses three slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.

WX-78 will begin spinning when using axes to cut down Trees and pickaxes to break Boulders. They can move around slowly while spinning and will work on all appropriate resources nearby; hitting the wrong type of resource (e.g. hitting a Boulder while holding an axe) will stop the spin. Additionally, they will perform a spin when harvesting all harvestable plants such as Grass and Saplings with a tool in hand. This also works on Cacti, which is useful to avoid taking damage when harvesting them.

Continuous spinning will make WX-78 increasingly dizzy (indicated by more spirals appearing over their head), and spinning too long at a time will daze them momentarily. With one Spin-Cycle Circuit, WX-78 can spin for 8 seconds before being dazed; with a second one, this increases to 12 seconds.

Attacking an enemy with an axe or pickaxe in hand will also cause WX-78 to spin, performing an area-of-effect attack that repeatedly deals the tool's full damage to nearby enemies. Notably, WX-78 does not flinch when hit while spinning.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering II

For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering II", the spinning speed of Spin Cycle Circuit increases.

Trivia

  • Circuits were introduced in the WX-78 Refresh as part of the character overhaul for Don't Starve Together.
  • More Circuits were introduced in the WX-78 Skill Spotlight and expand circuit board to have 3 type of circuit bars.
  • The Chorusbox Melody is based on Ragtime, the song that plays on the Gramaphone in the Epilogue of Adventure Mode. This is alluded to in Maxwell's quote for the Chorusbox Circuit.

plainText:
Exclusive to:
WX-78's Chassis Body
Circuits are WX-78-exclusive items craftable in the Survivor Items Filter in Don't Starve Together. All the circuits are unlocked by scanning certain mobs with Bio Scanalyzer. They can be categorized into three types: Alpha (red), Beta (blue) and Gamma (yellow). Each circuit corresponds for which bar it belongs to, based on what shapes are on top of the head, the color slab and pins.
When a Circuit is applied to the bar, it will attempt to take up the lowest unoccupied pins available. Removing a Circuit via Circuit Extractor will remove the highest Circuit and lose 25% durability.
When hovering over the charge meter, it will show which circuits are currently plugged in. The charge meter affects all three bars; when charge is gained or lost then this will activate or deactivate all three circuits in line with that charge.
With "Right To Modify" skill, WX-78 can remove any circuits in the bar stack. If two of the same circuits are next to each other, only the top one is able to be extracted. When extracted, circuits lose 12.5% instead of 25%.
With "Watts Up" skill, Circuits with 2 or more notches will take one less charge when extracted. Also, charge regenerate 3 times faster, taking thirty seconds per charge.
With "Off By One" skill, the Alpha, Beta and Gamma bars get an additional +1 slot each.

-
The Redigestion Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Catcoon. The Redigestion Circuit uses two slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board. With a single Circuit plugged in, when WX-78 consumes 5 Rot, Rotten Egg, Spoiled Fish or Spoiled Fish Morsel, they bake a Nutribrick. The cost can be reduced by 1 for each additional circuit plugged, down to 3 items to produce a Nutribrick with 3 Redigestion Circuits.
Gamma Circuits Tinkering I[edit | edit source]
For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering I", Redigestion Circuit can process food when it's Spoiled (red value), and contribute to baking the Nutribrick. Additionally, when spoiled, foods no longer give negative stats when processed.

The Chessmaster Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Clockwork Knight, Damaged Knight, or Gilded Knight. The Chessmaster Circuit uses a single slot and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.
Without Chessmaster Circuit, WX-78 can recruit 9 total clockworks, and max cap per type is 3.
Each Chessmaster Circuit increases +1 total clockworks.
Every 3 Chessmaster Circuits increases +1 max cap per type.
1-3 circuits => max cap per type is 4.
4-6 circuits => max cap per type is 5.
7 circuits => max cap per type is 6.
With 7 circuits, WX-78 can recruit a total of 9+7=16 clockworks, but no more than 6 of one type.  E.g.
6 bishops, 6 rooks, 4 knights
Or 6 bishops, 5 rooks, 5 knights
Extra clockworks can be recruited, but they will stay in place and wait. Once WX-78 has less than cap amount of clockworks, they will wake up and follow WX-78.
Gamma Circuits Tinkering I[edit | edit source]
For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering I" skill, plugging in a Chessmaster Circuit causes all Clockwork enemies to no longer attack unless attacked first.

-
The Blocking Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Slurtle, a Snurtle, or a Rock Lobster. The Blocking Circuit uses four slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.
When plugged in and powered, player can right-click to block. This will provide 80% defense and protect them against thief sources such as Frogs, Bearger, Powder Monkey, Otter, etc.
Blocking can be ended when WX-78 moves or it forcibly ends once more than 100 total damage has been taken (i.e. WX-78 loses more than 100 health). Additionally, taking damage or blocking for more than 5 seconds causes the ability to enter a 20-second cooldown.
This 80% protection only counts for Physical Damage. When paired with other armor, the armor takes damage first, and the rest of damage is further reduced by the Blocking Circuit. For example, an spider deals 20 damage. When WX-78 is blocking with Blocking Circuit and a Thulecite Suit:
20 * 0.9 = 18: Thulecite Suit reduces 18 durability.
(20 - 18) * (1 - 0.8) = 0.4: WX-78 loses 0.4 health.
Gamma Circuits Tinkering II[edit | edit source]
For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering II", Blocking Circuit gives immunity to Knockback.

Gamma Circuits Tinkering II makes WX-78 spin faster.”
-
The Spin-Cycle Circuit can be crafted after scanning a Mosling. The Spin-Cycle Circuit uses three slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board.
WX-78 will begin spinning when using axes to cut down Trees and pickaxes to break Boulders. They can move around slowly while spinning and will work on all appropriate resources nearby; hitting the wrong type of resource (e.g. hitting a Boulder while holding an axe) will stop the spin. Additionally, they will perform a spin when harvesting all harvestable plants such as Grass and Saplings with a tool in hand. This also works on Cacti, which is useful to avoid taking damage when harvesting them.
Continuous spinning will make WX-78 increasingly dizzy (indicated by more spirals appearing over their head), and spinning too long at a time will daze them momentarily. With one Spin-Cycle Circuit, WX-78 can spin for 8 seconds before being dazed; with a second one, this increases to 12 seconds.
Attacking an enemy with an axe or pickaxe in hand will also cause WX-78 to spin, performing an area-of-effect attack that repeatedly deals the tool's full damage to nearby enemies. Notably, WX-78 does not flinch when hit while spinning.
Gamma Circuits Tinkering II[edit | edit source]
For WX-78 with "Gamma Circuits Tinkering II", the spinning speed of Spin Cycle Circuit increases.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Circuits were introduced in the WX-78 Refresh as part of the character overhaul for Don't Starve Together.
More Circuits were introduced in the WX-78 Skill Spotlight and expand circuit board to have 3 type of circuit bars.
The Chorusbox Melody is based on Ragtime, the song that plays on the Gramaphone in the Epilogue of Adventure Mode. This is alluded to in Maxwell's quote for the Chorusbox Circuit.

title: Circuits
wikitext:
{{Exclusivity|Don't Starve Together}} [[File:WX-78 Chest UI.png|thumb|241x241px|WX-78's Chassis Body]] '''Circuits''' are [[WX-78/Don't Starve Together|WX-78]]-exclusive [[items]] craftable in the [[Survivor Items Filter]] in ''[[Don't Starve Together]]''. All the circuits are unlocked by scanning certain mobs with [[Bio Scanalyzer]]. They can be categorized into three types: Alpha (red), Beta (blue) and Gamma (yellow). Each circuit corresponds for which bar it belongs to, based on what shapes are on top of the head, the color slab and pins. When a Circuit is applied to the bar, it will attempt to take up the lowest unoccupied pins available. Removing a Circuit via [[Circuit Extractor]] will remove the highest Circuit and lose 25% durability. When hovering over the charge meter, it will show which circuits are currently plugged in. The charge meter affects all three bars; when charge is gained or lost then this will activate or deactivate all three circuits in line with that charge. With "{{PicDark|32|Right To Modify}}[[Right To Modify]]" skill, WX-78 can remove any circuits in the bar stack. If two of the same circuits are next to each other, only the top one is able to be extracted. When extracted, circuits lose 12.5% instead of 25%. With "{{PicDark|32|Watts Up}}[[Watts Up]]" skill, Circuits with 2 or more notches will take one less charge when extracted. Also, charge regenerate 3 times faster, taking thirty seconds per charge. With "{{PicDark|32|Off By One}}[[Off By One]]" skill, the Alpha, Beta and Gamma bars get an additional +1 slot each. {{TocLimit|2}} == Hardy Circuit == {{Object Infobox |name = Hardy Circuit |image = Hardy Circuit Dropped.png |icon = Hardy Circuit.png |ingredient1 = Bio Data |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Spider Gland |multiplier2 = 1 ({{pic24|WX-78 Portrait}} only) |stack = Does not stack |perk = Grants 40 maximum hp from a single slot. |spawnCode = "wx78module_maxhealth" |description = Make your robotic body a bit more robust. |durability = 4 uses |filter = {{Pic32|WX-78 Filter}}}} {{Quotes |wilson = So much science packed into one tiny gizmo. |willow = Hey WX, when are you gonna install a flamethrower? |wolfgang = Is robot snacks, yes? |wendy = It looks so fragile... hopefully no tragic accidents befall it. |wx78 = IT IS DIFFICULT TO IMPROVE ON PERFECTION, BUT I PERSEVERED |wickerbottom = They dislike organics, but don't seem opposed to taking inspiration from them. |woodie = Some fancy robot bits. |maxwell = That robot needs to pull themselves together. |wigfrid = These bööns shall empöwer öur metal warriör. |webber = Huh. Gamma Circuits Tinkering II makes WX-78 spin faster.}} The '''Spin-Cycle Circuit''' can be crafted after scanning a [[Mosling/DST|Mosling]]. The Spin-Cycle Circuit uses three slots and plugs into the Gamma circuit board. WX-78 will begin spinning when using axes to cut down [[Tree|Trees]] and pickaxes to break [[Boulder/DST|Boulders]]. They can move around slowly while spinning and will work on all appropriate resources nearby; hitting the wrong type of resource (e.g. hitting a Boulder while holding an axe) will stop the spin. Additionally, they will perform a spin when harvesting all harvestable plants such as [[Grass/DST|Grass]] and [[Sapling/DST|Saplings]] with a tool in hand. This also works on [[Cactus/DST|Cacti]], which is useful to avoid taking damage when harvesting them. Continuous spinning will make WX-78 increasingly dizzy (indicated by more spirals appearing over their head), and spinning too long at a time will daze them momentarily. With one Spin-Cycle Circuit, WX-78 can spin for 8 seconds before being dazed; with a second one, this increases to 12 seconds. Attacking an enemy with an axe or pickaxe in hand will also cause WX-78 to spin, performing an area-of-effect attack that repeatedly deals the tool's full damage to nearby enemies. Notably, WX-78 does not flinch when hit while spinning. ==={{PicDark|32|Gamma Circuits Tinkering II}} Gamma Circuits Tinkering II=== For WX-78 with "[[Gamma Circuits Tinkering II]]", the spinning speed of Spin Cycle Circuit increases. {{clear}} =={{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia== * Circuits were introduced in the [[WX-78 Refresh]] as part of the character overhaul for ''Don't Starve Together''. * More Circuits were introduced in the [[WX-78 Skill Spotlight]] and expand circuit board to have 3 type of circuit bars. * The Chorusbox Melody is based on ''[[Soundtrack|Ragtime]]'', the song that plays on the [[Gramaphone]] in the Epilogue of [[Adventure Mode]].
